items.title: Caregiver Support Hotline
items.description: 24/7 phone support for caregivers in crisis or feeling overwhelmed. You are not alone — call any time.

items.title: When to Seek Emergency Care
items.description: A quick guide to recognizing medical emergencies in loved ones with dementia, and what to tell first responders.

items.title: Understanding Younger-Onset Dementia
items.description: What families should know when dementia arrives early — symptoms, progression, and how to plan together.

items.title: Preparing for a Memory Social
items.description: What to expect from drop-in respite, how to prepare your loved one for a visit, and how to make the most of your break.

items.title: Video: Communication Techniques
items.description: Practical ways to connect when words get hard — redirection, validation, and meeting your loved one where they are.

items.title: Wellness Grants: How to Apply
items.description: Who's eligible, what grants can fund — massage, counseling, a gym membership — and how awards and care packages are personalized.

items.title: 10-Minute Reset Practices
items.description: Breathing, grounding, and micro-break practices that fit inside a caregiving day.

items.title: Virtual Support Group (Wednesdays)
items.description: Our Alzheimer's Association-certified weekly group — Wednesdays 5:00–6:00 PM CST via Google Meet. Judgment-free, caregiver-only.

items.title: Memory Social Caregiver Events
items.description: While your loved one enjoys a supervised Memory Social, join fellow caregivers for wellness, connection, and education — side by side in the same space.

items.title: Benefits & Assistance Finder
items.description: A starting map of public benefits, veteran programs, and assistance funds available to family caregivers in our region.

items.title: Planning Ahead: POA & Care Decisions
items.description: Plain-language basics on powers of attorney, advance directives, and getting documents in order early.
